perturbation.original_text: 1.91 "Sales Representative" means a sales representative used by Company to perform Details of the Product for the Initial Indication to Health Care Professionals in the Territory. Sales Representative shall not include any key account manager, medical science liaison or regional sales manager.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.91 "Sales Representative" means a sales representative used by Company to perform Details of the Product for the Initial Indication to Health Care Professionals in the Territory. Sales Representative may include key account managers, medical science liaisons or regional sales managers, at Company's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Sales Representative" explicitly excludes key account managers, medical science liaisons, and regional sales managers. The modified definition states that those positions may be included at the Company's discretion. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 3.3.1, which uses the term, relies on the original narrow definition. By broadening the definition, the clause is contradictory when interpreting the obligations and qualifications of the Sales Representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.5 Sales Representatives and Other Company Employees. 11.5.1 Company covenants to Janssen that: (a) with respect to the Product, the Sales Representatives and Other Company Employees in the Territory shall make no statements, claims, or undertakings to any health care provider with whom they discuss or promote the Product that are not consistent with, nor provide nor use any labeling, literature, or other materials other than, the Product Label and Insert and those Promotional Materials provided and approved for use pursuant to this Agreement; and (b) it shall ensure that all statements, comments and claims made by the Sales Representatives and Other Company Employees (i) about the Product, including as to efficacy and safety, are truthful and accurate and are consistent with and in strict compliance with the Product Label and Insert and all applicable Laws, and (ii) about Janssen in relation to the Product are truthful, accurate, and in strict compliance with all applicable Laws. Any statement, claim or comment that is contained in the Promotional Materials or the Product Label and Insert, in each case, as in effect when such statement, claim or comment is made, shall be deemed not to violate this Section 11.5.1.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Sales Representative" to *exclude* specific roles. The changed text modifies this to *include* those roles at the Company's discretion. Section 11.5 relies on the original, narrower definition, requiring "Sales Representatives and Other Company Employees" to adhere to specific guidelines when promoting the product. By broadening the definition of "Sales Representative," the obligation in 11.5 becomes unclear. It introduces uncertainty as to which roles fall under the "Sales Representatives" category and which fall under "Other Company Employees", thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated, shall expire on the Expiration Date (the "Term"). For the avoidance of doubt, the Parties agree that Janssen's obligations under Section 5.2 (subject to Section 8.2.5) and Section 5.3 (subject to Section 8.2.5) shall remain in effect beyond the Expiration Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated, shall expire on the Expiration Date (the "Term"). For the avoidance of doubt, the Parties agree that Janssen's obligations under Section 5.2 (subject to Section 8.2.5) and Section 5.3 (subject to Section 8.2.5) shall not remain in effect beyond the Expiration Date.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that Janssen's obligations under Sections 5.2 and 5.3 (Service Fees and Milestones) will continue beyond the Expiration Date. The change reverses this statement, asserting that these obligations will *not* continue after the Expiration Date. This introduces a direct in-text contradiction because the parties are not clear on their financial duties after the end of the term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2.6 Survival. Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve a Party of any liability for any breach that occurred, or of any obligation to make payment that accrued, before or on the effective date of such termination or expiration, nor prejudice either Party's right to obtain performance of any obligation provided for in this Agreement that survives termination or expiration. All provisions of this Agreement which, in accordance with their terms, are intended to have effect after the expiration or termination of this Agreement shall survive such termination or expiration, including: Sections 3.1.2, 3.2.4(a) (with respect to the last month of the Term), 5.2 (subject to Section 8.2.5), 5.3 (subject to Section 8.2.5), 5.5.1, 5.5.2, 5.5.5, 5.5.6, 5.6, 7.1, 7.2, 8.1.1 (last sentence only), 8.1.10 (only if the Agreement is terminated pursuant to Section 8.1.10), 8.2, 9.1, 10.2, 11.8, and Articles XII, XIII and XIV.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8.1.1 states that Janssen's obligations under Sections 5.2 and 5.3 will *not* continue after the Expiration Date. This directly contradicts Section 8.2.6, which explicitly lists Sections 5.2 and 5.3 as provisions that survive termination or expiration of the agreement. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the parties' financial obligations after the contract's term, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 9.2 Exclusivity. During the Term, neither Company nor any of its Affiliates (including, for the avoidance of doubt, any Third Party that becomes an Affiliate of Company after the Effective Date) shall, alone or in collaboration with any Third Party, market, promote, sell, distribute or otherwise commercialize in the Territory any Competing Product without the prior written consent of Janssen.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.2 Exclusivity. During the Term, Company and its Affiliates may market, promote, sell, distribute or otherwise commercialize in the Territory any Competing Product without the prior written consent of Janssen, unless such activity is expressly prohibited in writing by Janssen.
perturbation.explanation: The original version of this section strictly prohibits Company from commercializing any competing product without Janssen's prior written consent. The modified version permits such activities unless Janssen explicitly prohibits them in writing. This contradicts the initial intention of the exclusivity clause, creating uncertainty about Company's freedom to engage with competing products. This creates an In-Text Contradiction with Section 8.1.6 which stipulates that Janssen can terminate this agreement at anytime if the Company commits a breach of Section 9.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1.6 Competing Products. In the event that Company commits a breach of Section 9.2 or Section 9.3, then Janssen shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in its entirety at any time immediately upon written notice to Company.
perturbation.justification: The modified version of Section 9.2 permits the Company to commercialize competing products unless expressly prohibited by Janssen, directly contradicting the original version which strictly prohibited such activities without Janssen's prior written consent. This creates a strong contradiction with Section 8.1.6, which allows Janssen to terminate the agreement if the Company breaches Section 9.2, because now it's unclear under what circumstances the Company would be in breach, rendering Section 8.1.6 ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.
